chunk_summary: Loss-of-control eating is a critical comorbidity in obesity management requiring weight-neutral stabilization strategies and mental health evaluation to prevent binge cycles and distress.
learning_objectives: Understand the clinical features and screening methods for loss-of-control (LOC) eating in children and adolescents.
Learn weight-neutral stabilization strategies for managing LOC eating and associated mood disorders.
Identify triggers and compensatory behaviors linked to LOC eating and their impact on obesity treatment outcomes.
key_facts: Loss-of-control eating involves a subjective inability to stop eating, even if the amount consumed is not objectively large.
Screening should assess frequency (e.g., weekly vs. rare), triggers (e.g., stress, bullying), and compensatory behaviors (e.g., vomiting, laxatives).
LOC eating often co-occurs with mood disorders, weight stigma, and suicidality, requiring mental health evaluation.
Weight-neutral stabilization includes regular meals/snacks, sleep regulation, and psychotherapy (CBT or family-based interventions).
Avoid aggressive weight-loss messaging and restrictive diets, as these can amplify binge cycles.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Binge eating disorder in children and adolescents requires evidence-based psychotherapy such as CBT or family-based interventions. (NICE guideline NG69)
document_key_topics: Mental health evaluation in obesity management
